caption:
When workouts start feeling heavier and performance plateaus despite doing “everything right”, that’s not random.

Elevated hsCRP can signal ongoing inflammation.
Low ferritin can limit oxygen delivery to muscle.
Hormone shifts can affects strength, drive, and adaptation.

Sometimes poor recovery isn’t a motivation problem. It’s often a biological signal that most just aren’t measuring.
hookLine: When workouts start feeling heavier and performance plateaus despite doing “everything right”, that’s not random.

caption:
He looks 34.
His biology says otherwise.

Tyler trains, eats well, and even looks like he does everything “right”. But on the inside, his body is working overtime.

Hormonal imbalances and deficiencies that may seem minor now could be silently building and accelerating aging beneath the surface. 

A simple blood draw can catch it early and adjust your future.
hookLine: He looks 34.

caption:
** GIVEAWAY CLOSED ** 

We’ve teamed up with our friends @mitohealthapp to give you the kind of health insights that usually stay locked behind clinic doors. 

One lucky winner will receive: 

* A Core Biomarker Panel that gives you personalized insights into your metabolism, inflammation, cardiovascular markers, and more 

* A full Allergy Panel to uncover hidden sensitivities and triggers 

* A Molekule Air Mini+ with PECO Technology, our award-winning air purifier designed to trap and destroy airborne allergens at home 

This giveaway is for anyone ready to take health into their own hands, whether you’re on a wellness journey, seeking better sleep, or just want to stop guessing and start knowing. 

The best part? You’ll get a detailed, science-backed snapshot of what’s happening inside your body, and the air you breathe. 

To enter: Like this post, follow @molekuleair and @mitohealthapp, and tag a friend. Open to U.S. residents only. We’ll choose a winner on August 12, 2025.
hookLine: ** GIVEAWAY CLOSED **

caption:
😓 An unhealthy diet can have profound, far-reaching consequences on your overall health and longevity. 

Consistently consuming a diet lacking essential nutrients or full of harmful substances (as listed above) can weaken your immune system, making you more vulnerable to infections and illnesses. 

Over time, poor dietary habits can lead to chronic diseases such as heart disease, diabetes, hypertension, and obesity, each of which can severely impact your quality of life in the long run.

Moreover, the effects of poor nutrition extend to mental health, often exacerbating conditions like depression, anxiety, and cognitive decline. 

Without the right balance of vitamins, minerals, and other nutrients, your body and mind cannot function at their best.

Remember, nutrition is a cornerstone of preventive health. By prioritising a balanced diet rich in fruits, vegetables, lean proteins, and whole grains, you are investing in your long-term health and well-being. 

Small, mindful changes in your eating habits today can lead to significant improvements in your health tomorrow. 

Make the choice to nourish your body with the nutrients it needs and deserve. 

Stay tuned for our next post, where we explore a key strategy used for eating right - the Mediterranean diet!

–
🌴 Screen your health now at our new oasis: Trapeze Rec. Club. Reserve your slots by clicking the link in bio.
–
☎️ Link in bio to book a free consultation call with Mito’s longevity doctors.
–
#healthspan #longevity #biohacking #nutrition
hookLine: 😓 An unhealthy diet can have profound, far-reaching consequences on your overall health and longevity.

caption:
Your dad gave you life - and this Father’s Day, let him know how much you care.

As we age, our bodies become more and more susceptible to many illnesses and chronic diseases. It’s hard to predict or prevent them too - after all, so many of them barely manifest any clinical symptoms. 

What better way to proactively prevent such silent killers from taking root, than by screening your dad’s health with Mito?

Mito’s innovative at-home Flagship Package measures heart health, hormone levels, nutrient deficiencies and more - all with a few drops of blood. It provides comprehensive insights into his wellbeing, allowing him to take proactive steps towards living healthier and happier!

This one’s for dad - it’s your turn to give him the power to create a healthier life for himself.

caption:
What makes Mito Screenings different?

Our goal is simple - comprehensive, longevity-focused biomarker testing at the most accessible price point. Our flagship package includes curated biomarkers like ApoB, Lp(a), insulin, homocysteine, and Cystatin-C, uncovering layers of your health that standard screenings may not cover.

Not only advanced biomarkers - we also revolutionise your health journey to be seamless, simple, and sustainable. Experience seamless home-based screenings or visit Trapeze Rec Club in Tanjong Pagar for unparalleled convenience (done within 15 minutes). Navigate your health data effortlessly with our AI-powered Health Dashboard, vetted by our Chief Medical Officer, Dr. Ryan, ensuring you’re always aligned with the latest in longevity science. Track progress, adjust your personalised health plan, and unlock member-exclusive benefits and longevity events.

Don’t settle. Join Mito Health, and let’s embark on a journey to optimal health, informed by the latest science, and tailored just for you.

primary.textOverlay:
Biomarker:
/'baiə(ʊ)ˌmɑːkə/

① Measurable indicators in biological samples
(blood, urine, tissues).

② Used to assess your body's processes,
presence of disease, illness progression, or
response to treatments.

primary.prompt: A clean, minimalist editorial image featuring a light grey background. A large, bold black testimonial quote is prominently displayed on the left side, while a professional, well-lit portrait of a smiling Asian man in a light polo shirt is positioned on the right. A subtle horizontal divider line is below the portrait, with smaller black text providing the name and title. Soft, even studio lighting. High resolution, clear typography.

caption:
Our brand name 'Mito' is short for Mitochondria which are the powerhouses of your cells!

They play a vital role in cellular respiration; which is to use oxygen to break down food molecules into energy for cell functions.

Paradoxically, there is evidence that mild mitochondria stress (such as through exercising) triggers a series of response that protects the organism from pathogens, so that it lives longer.

Just like the Mitochondria's fascinating link with life expectancy, Mito's here to help you achieve peak health by screening a panel of 63 advanced biomarkers in you, each curated with longevity in mind.

--

Start 2024 with a splash! Get a cold bath & sauna at Trapeze*, when you book a Mito health scan by 9th Feb'24. 

#LongerHealthierLife #LiveBetterwithMito #Helathspan *T&Cs apply.
hookLine: Our brand name 'Mito' is short for Mitochondria which are the powerhouses of your cells!

primary.composition: tipografia estruturada em um painel retangular de cantos arredondados sobre um fundo escuro com formas orgânicas e desfocadas
primary.subject: tipografia pura
primary.textOverlay:
Mito:
[mai-toe] The Secret to Longevity
① Short for 'Mitochondria'
② Mitochondria are membrane-bound cell organelles (mitochondrion, singular) that generate most of the chemical energy needed to power the cell's biochemical reactions.
Chemical energy produced by the mitochondria is stored in a small molecule called adenosine triphosphate (ATP).
Source: National Human Genome Institute
primary.hookSignal: O título grande e a frase intrigante 'The Secret to Longevity' capturam a atenção.
primary.prompt: Modern minimalist infographic design, featuring a central rounded rectangle card with structured textual information, bold sans-serif typography. The main title is large and prominent. Text is crisp white on a dark charcoal-blue background, with subtle light grey separator lines. The background behind the card is a blurred, dark gradient with abstract, organic shapes. Clean, professional lighting, editorial style.
